description_long: This object consists of a group of outdoor air conditioning condenser units, each with a rectangular metal housing and visible fan grilles on the front or top. The units vary in size and configuration, with some featuring dual circular fans and others a single large fan. The exteriors are predominantly a light beige or off-white color, with black or dark gray fan blades and grille elements. Several units display horizontal or vertical slatted grilles for air intake and exhaust, and a few have small colored labels or logos on the front. The bases are reinforced with black mounting brackets, suggesting installation on flat surfaces such as rooftops or ground pads. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on durability and efficient airflow management.
description: A group of rectangular, beige outdoor air conditioner condenser units with visible fan grilles, slatted vents, and black mounting bases. The units feature both single and dual fan designs and display utilitarian, industrial styling.
description_view_0: Front view showing the fan grilles, slatted vents, and small colored labels on the face of each unit.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the units, the arrangement of the mounting bases, and the side ventilation grilles.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid backs and the mounting brackets, with no visible fans or logos.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the side grilles and the overall boxy shape of the units.
attribute_brand_logo: Small colored rectangular logos (blue and red) are visible on the front of two units, positioned in the upper right corner. The logos are simple and lack detailed branding.
attribute_grille_pattern: The grilles feature horizontal and vertical slats, with some units having circular fan grilles with radial spokes and others rectangular slatted intake/output vents. Spacing is moderate, allowing airflow while protecting internal components.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Exhaust vents are located on the front and sides, with horizontal or vertical slats and circular fan openings. Some units have dual circular vents on top, while others have a single large front-facing vent.
